授業目的: 本講義の目的は、学生が主体的に、古代から現代に至る日本の伝統芸能の歴史や特徴を学ぶとともに、芸能が果たした文化史の面での意義や社会的な役割についても考察する。
到達目標:
The students will gain a comprehensive knowledge of Japanese traditional
 performance culture and deepen their understanding of both the 
historical and social factors that formed the various performing art 
forms, and the intricate workings of power, cultural representation, and 
gender.
特記事項: 【合併】総合日本学習科目（伝統芸能）（CIEC）/Traditional Japanese Theatre（国際学部）
関連科目:
There is no prerequisite for this course but a related course would be 
any standard course on Japanese culture, history and literature.
授業の概要・背景:
This course introduces students to Japanese traditional performing arts, in the broad sense of the term performance: as a 
cultural representation literally imbued with meanings, both artistic 
and social. We examine the main stage arts not only as forms of 
entertainment but also in their historical, social and cultural 
contexts: the early forms kagura and bugaku, the classical noh drama and
 the kyogen comedy, the puppet theatre bunraku, the actor’s theatre 
kabuki, the all-female Takarazuka Revue, geisha performances, and various folk genres. 
Special attention is paid to the performance of gender and the body in 
Japanese culture, and particularly to the female performers in the 
predominantly male theatrical tradition. The course provides the 
students with a firm foundation from which a deeper understanding of the
 role of performing arts in Japanese culture and society is built.

第1回:
Startup Week 
Online class (On-demand format) 《30 minutes》
Guidance. Introduction to Japanese theatre. Overview of Japanese Cultural History.
第2回:
Core Week 1 - April 10
In-Person Class  《90 minutes》
Sources of Japanese theatre I: origins, history, early performing art forms - kagura; folk performing arts.
第3回:
Core Week 2 - April 17
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Sources of Japanese theatre II: origins, history, early performing art forms - bugaku; folk performing arts.
第4回:
Core Week 3 - April 24
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Kabuki - actor's theatre, origins and history, stage conventions, types of plays, elements of performance.
第5回:
Core Week 4 - May 1 (No classes May 8, May 15)
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Kabuki - gender presentation - role types, onnagata's body, masculinity, screening of plays, discussion.
Preparation for kabuki performance observation (May 16 or 17).

30-40 minutes. Reading and visual materials.
May 16 (Saturday) or 17 (Sunday) Kabuki performance observation, Minami-za, Kyoto (TBC)
第6回:
Core Week 5 - May 22
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Noh theatre: history and development, audiences, elements of performance, types of plays. Noh workshop by KGU Noh Student Club.
No class on May 15 (Friday) - instead - fieldtrip with instructor to watch Noh performance on June 6.
第7回:
Core Week 6 - May 29
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Kyogen comedy: types of kyogen, elements of performance, stories.
第8回:
Core Week 7 - June 5
In-Person Class《90 minutes》
Super Kabuki. Manga and anime in Kabuki and Noh. Contemporary Noh plays. 
Preparation for noh performance observation on June 6 (Saturday).
第9回:
Fieldtrip with instructor - June 6 (Saturday)
In-Person Class 《240 minutes》
Noh performance observation - Kanze Kaikan Noh Theatre, Kyoto (Nonomiya, Adachigahara).
Noh theatre observation in Kanze Kaikan Noh Theatre, Kyoto. June 6 (Saturday).
第10回:
Core Week 8 - June 12
In-Person Class《90 minutes》
Bunraku puppet theatre: elements of performance. Screening of bunraku plays.
Preparation for the performance observation at the National Bunraku Theatre in Osaka on June 14.
June 14 (Sunday) - Bunraku puppet theatre performance observation at the National Bunraku Theatre in Osaka (14:30 - 16:45). 1,600 yen.
第11回:
Core Week 9 - June 19
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Women in Japanese performing arts: a historical overview. Female versions of all-male performing arts. Geisha performance.
第12回:
Core Week 10 - June 26
In-Person Class《90 minutes》
The all-female Takarazuka Review. Screening of a play. Butoh.
第13回:
Core Week 11 - July 3
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Student project presentations.
第14回:
Core Week 12 - July 10
In-Person Class 《90 minutes》
Student project presentations.

参考文献・資料/Reference books0: Jonah Salz (ed.), "A History of Japanese  Theatre", Cambridge University Press, 2018 ISNB: 978-1108458160
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: 授業外学修課題／Assignment, homework required outside class (06)

Students are required to leave comments/questions/impressions on class material in the respective discussion forum on LUNA, and to submit a response paper on the bunraku or another performance they have watched.
成績評価Grading2: プレゼンテーション・発表／Presentation (07)
Originality, logical structure, research abilities, proper use of citation and reference materials, persuasive conclusion, presentation skills.
成績評価Grading3: 備考　Note 																																																Final Project Presentation (60%): Students are required to do additional research on a topicrelated to the class content and prepare a final presentation.

name: 【310】Traditional Japanese Theatre／Traditional Japanese Theatre [EN]
担当者: ペトコヴァ　ガリア（PETKOVA GALIA）
